creator: olivierd@FreeBSD.org
text:
It's not recommended to build with GTK3 support. They're lot of bugs (mostly fixed by upstream, but I'm not sure if changes are compatible with your outdated version of Gtk+3).

Remainder: Midori was updated on August 2015 (just after upgrade of GNOME3 from 3.14 to 3.16 as it depends of Gtk+ 2/3 and WebKitGtk). Everything was fine at the time, but now we have Gtk+ 3.18 and with this update some functions are deprecated or removed, moreover Midori has not been tested with GTK3 support (it's not default option), so for me Midori is not broken.
